description: Duck somewhat resembling female mallard. Body darker than that of mallard, mottled black and rich brown. Chest slightly darker and vent slightly paler than remainder of underparts. Head and neck quite contrastingly paler, being a pale tan-like color with fine streaking throughout except on chin, which was slightly peachy in color. Crown blackish. Blackish eyeline. Tail gray. No curled tail feathers. Spread underwing like that of Mallard, without dusky markings of Mottled Duck or Am Black Duck. Speculum iridescent, usually appearing bluer than more purple speculum of Mallard. Narrower white borders, anteriorly and posteriorly, than that of surrounding Mallards. Bill entirely yellow-orange, without darker markings. Size of male MallardBehaviors: Feeding with American Wigeon and Mallards at park pondCall: not heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow
